subject: Project Management
title: The Baldrige Award
description: A fast breakdown of The Baldrige Award for builders.

hook.text: In 1987, the U.S. created the Malcolm Baldrige National Quality Award to match Japan's Deming Prize.

buildup.text: The award evaluates organizations across seven categories: leadership, strategy, customers, and more.

discovery.text: Baldrige winners consistently outperform the S&P 500 — proof that quality excellence drives financial results.

twist.text: Most companies don't apply to win. They use the Baldrige criteria as a self-assessment tool for improvement.

climax.text: The Baldrige Award made quality a competitive weapon — not just a cost of doing business.

punchline.text: America's quality prize taught companies to compete on quality, not just price.

quiz.question: How do Baldrige Award winners perform financially?
options: They have higher costs than competitors
